previous_effect: SteerBar had its own Redirect input plus a Steer button. The gesture fired Intent::Custom{redirect_run}, which the host recorded as a custom event and acted on in no way. rpc turn/steer returned NotImplemented and the InterruptHub Steer variant was never signalled, so mid turn steering had zero effect end to end.
new_effect: The composer itself steers: while a run is in flight Enter resolves the catalog command steer, bound Custom(redirect_run), and host.rs steer_action raises a real InterruptHub::Steer next to the already wired Abort, Pause and Resume. The same command is what a palette search result attach and a hunk alternative request use, so one capability has one route.
panel_changes: SteerBar keeps the run phase and the lifecycle verbs (pause, resume, cancel) and loses its second text box. No panel added, no panel removed.
keyboard_available: Enter while a run is live (the default action the composer label names), Mod+/ explicitly, Mod+Enter to start a separate turn instead. The catalog owns the shortcut through composer.steer.
reversal_available: cancel_run and pause_run still address the same run id. A steer itself is additive text, so there is nothing to undo.
evidence_visible: The composer state label reads Steer run while a turn is in flight and Start turn otherwise, and the menu entry states why it cannot fire when there is no run.
duplicate_entry_removed: the SteerBar redirect input, a second text box that meant the same thing as the composer
errors_prevented: typing a redirect into a box that discarded it; two text boxes with different meanings for one capability
still_unfinished: none for the wire. What the model does with a steer mid turn is model dependent and is not asserted here.

previous_effect: CodeActions offered explain and write-tests, which inlined 600 characters of the selection into a submit_turn prompt, and refactor, which fired Intent::Custom{inline_edit} with no host handler at all. A header comment advertised a fourth action (fork and try 3 via fleet_run) that never rendered. There was no way to open a side chat about a selection, and no check that the selection still said what it said when it was taken.
new_effect: A selection resolves to a stable SourceRef (path, line range, content hash) that is re-read from the live buffer before every dispatch, so a moved or edited selection refuses instead of citing lines that no longer exist. The menu dispatches catalog commands only: submit_turn for ask, explain, plan, test and verify, create_side_chat for a review side chat, run_command for line history, and the ONE search engine for references and attach.
panel_changes: none. The menu is anchored to the selection and opened by selecting code or Shift+F10, so no permanent editor control appears.
modal_changes: none. It is a popover, not a modal.
keyboard_available: Shift+F10 or the context menu key opens the menu from the keyboard; the palette reaches the same command ids.
reversal_available: None is needed: every entry asks the agent rather than writing to disk. Anything the agent then proposes is reversible through the diff review path (reject_diff, revert_diff).
evidence_visible: Every request cites path:startLine-endLine, and a stale ref is named as stale rather than sent.
duplicate_entry_removed: add to context (pin_span) merged into attach, since putting a selection in front of the model is one capability
errors_prevented: citing a selection that had moved or changed; a refactor gesture that logged and did nothing; an advertised action that never rendered
still_unfinished: The verify entry here ASKS the agent and says so. The deterministic checker is now reachable (run_static_analysis binds Custom and host.rs handle_static_analysis_intent serves it), but its one control is the StatusBar Problems counter, not a second button on this menu. Whether the agent produces a good refactor is model dependent.

previous_effect: One keystroke per hunk (a or r), but the intent carried no hunk id, so accepting one hunk fired a WHOLE diff accept and a second hunk accept re-fired the identical intent. Host side accept_diff and reject_diff were validated and durably logged with NO apply path, so even the whole diff accept changed nothing on disk. In chat, DiffChipRow rendered accept and reject buttons that never appeared because Conversation passed no handlers.
new_effect: accept_diff and reject_diff carry an additive optional hunk_id. With it the host runs apply_hunk or reject_hunk through the verifying applier; without it the same two names mean the whole diff (apply_diff, revert_diff). reject_hunk reverts exactly that file and appends verify.invalidated for every verification receipt whose scope covers it, which is why a rejected hunk then offers a re-verify.
panel_changes: The Editor diff bar stays at two controls in both phases (accept or reject the whole diff, then whole diff revert once every hunk is decided). The in-chat DiffChipRow keeps one open/review control. Each hunk header gained a detail toggle, which is one of the campaign's two genuinely new permanent visible elements.
modal_changes: none. The per-hunk detail is disclosed in place under the hunk header.
keyboard_available: j and k or arrows move, a or Mod+Enter accepts, r or Mod+Backspace rejects, m opens the hunk detail, d opens the review detail. Those bare keys only fire while focus is inside the diff surface and never inside an INPUT or TEXTAREA, and Escape only closes the hunk detail. The catalog owns accept_diff and reject_diff as shortcut-only rows (palette off, context menu on).
reversal_available: Yes, and it is real: an accepted hunk offers revert, a rejected hunk offers re-apply, and the whole diff offers revert_diff once every hunk is decided. Both directions route to the same host verbs, so one action never wears two controls.
evidence_visible: Each hunk shows its originating plan step, agent, turn and base hash when the host recorded them, and says so plainly when it did not. Status reads pending, working, accepted, rejected or failed in words. Add and delete lines carry a plus or minus marker as well as the pigment.
duplicate_entry_removed: the in-chat accept and reject pair that never rendered; edit_hunk was NOT bound to a second control because it routes to the same host apply_hunk as accept_diff{hunk_id}, and two controls for one action is the thing being removed
errors_prevented: accepting the whole diff while believing one hunk was accepted; a second accept silently re-firing the first; a review gesture that produced no host effect at all; a bare destructive key firing from a surface that was not the diff
safety_cost: Rejecting a hunk is marked destructive and states, before it is sent, that reverting the file invalidates every verification receipt whose scope covers it. That is a deliberate friction on a disk changing action.
still_unfinished: Rewind, fork an alternative and compare candidates all need a checkpoint_id that no diff UiEvent carries, so the reachable undo here is revert_diff plus per hunk re-apply. Re-verifying the affected files is reachable, but from the StatusBar Problems counter, not from a second control on this surface.

previous_effect: Enter dispatched run_command and got an ack, but there was NO live stdout: output only appeared as a side effect of the unrelated tool_progress echo. The command ran argv UNSANDBOXED through exec_command_streamed, bypassing the sandboxed shell.run path the agent uses, behind only a dangerous_command gate and a cwd check. The declared pty_input and pty_resize names were never used, so Ctrl+C reached nothing. Navigating away lost the view of the process.
new_effect: run_command resolves through the ONE spine and the host runs it through the sandboxed process surface (spawn_supervised then confine), which is FAIL CLOSED: with no OS sandbox it refuses rather than running unconfined, and the refusal is surfaced as a blocked process rather than as a claim that the command ran. Output streams incrementally, every new row written as it arrives. Ctrl+C writes 0x03 to the live process stdin through pty_input, and geometry goes out once per process through pty_resize.
panel_changes: One compact state row was added inside the existing panel: workspace env, cwd, sandbox state, process id and state, exit state, owning task. It carries no buttons. This is one of the campaign's two genuinely new permanent visible elements, and it exists because sandbox posture and cwd cannot be inferred from the scrollback. No new panel.
keyboard_available: Enter runs, Ctrl+C interrupts the live process. run_command is a catalog command, so the palette resolves the same semantics.
reversal_available: Ctrl+C interrupts. There is no undo for a command that already ran, which is correct.
evidence_visible: The state row reports what the host actually told us. The exit field reads not reported because the supervisor streams output but publishes no terminal status event yet, which is stated rather than guessed.
duplicate_entry_removed: the private exec path that duplicated, and undercut, the sandboxed tool route
errors_prevented: believing a command produced no output when the stream was simply not wired; running an interactive shell outside the sandbox the agent is held to; losing a running process by switching panels
safety_cost: A command can now be refused outright when no OS sandbox is available. That is a deliberate fail closed cost: fewer commands run, and the ones that do are confined.
still_unfinished: stop_process, attach_process, detach_process and capture_process_artifact exist on the host but NO command in the catalog reaches them, so this surface grows no button that would only log. No terminal status event exists yet, so exit state is honestly unknown.

previous_effect: Scrub fired scrub_to_event, which was validated and logged with no host apply path. A separate permanent fork from here button fired fork_session, which WAS real. ContextStack snapshot state, save skill and the three load skill rows were toast only mocks that persisted nothing. checkpoint_create, checkpoint_restore, checkpoint_rewind, replay, fork, compare and inspect were all implemented and integrity verified with blake3, and NO frontend control sent any of them.
new_effect: One history menu on the timeline row resolves ten verbs, each naming a catalog command and carrying the payload handle_goal_checkpoint_intent parses: create, fork from this step, inspect, compare, replay, fork from checkpoint, restore, and three explicitly targeted rewinds (conversation, code, both). A rewind with an omitted target is REFUSED by the host rather than defaulted to the widest domain, and a code or both rewind now actually reverts the working tree, rejecting every post boundary hunk newest first through the same verifying inverse write the diff reject path uses. The checkpoint verbs address store.lastCheckpointId, the id folded from the host checkpoint_created Custom UiEvent, not a scraped string.
panel_changes: none net. The separate permanent fork from here BUTTON was retired and one menu trigger took its slot, so this counts as a one-for-one swap: the row carries the same number of controls and nine more capabilities.
modal_changes: none. Destructive verbs arm in place with a second click; the source implements the two click guard and never a modal.
keyboard_available: Escape closes the menu and disarms; focus returns to the trigger that opened it. The palette reaches the same command ids.
reversal_available: This workflow IS the reversal surface for the rest of the app: restore opens a new session folded to the boundary and leaves the source untouched, replay re-applies forward and drops nothing, fork branches. Only the three rewinds destroy work, and each names exactly what it reverts.
evidence_visible: The menu header states the addressable checkpoint id, or no checkpoint yet. Every blocked entry states its reason in words. Every fired action reports working, accepted or the host refusal text. A code rewind reports the files it reverted and the receipts it invalidated.
duplicate_entry_removed: the standalone fork from here button, now one entry among ten in the same menu
errors_prevented: a bare rewind whose scope was ambiguous or silently defaulted to both; addressing a checkpoint that does not exist; a snapshot button that toasted success the host never gave; a rewind that rewrote history while leaving the reverted work on disk
safety_cost: Every rewind takes an explicit second, confirming click and is labelled reverts work then click again. checkpoint_restore and checkpoint_rewind also carry approval_policy ask, which the host now enforces at the intent boundary: the intent is recorded and the EFFECT is parked on a gate id, so the caller can tell held from done. That is one extra gesture plus one approval on the only genuinely destructive verbs in the set, taken deliberately.
still_unfinished: store.ts keeps the LAST checkpoint id, not a checkpoint LIST, so this surface cannot enumerate or diff checkpoints. A rewind detail.invalidated_receipts still falls past the 200 character truncation of the Custom info notice, so invalidated receipts cannot be listed here yet. The working tree revert has no transaction, so a conflict part way leaves the earlier files reverted (marked in source as a ponytail shortcut with its upgrade path, the same exposure revert_diff already has).

previous_effect: The Explorer field debounced a query to code_index.search with the payload {q, limit}, a shape the connector cannot deserialize (it takes {query: SearchQuery} and answers {results}, not a bare array), so EVERY keystroke fell through to the local filename walk and the panel had never once shown a real index hit. rpc item/list mapped to a real implemented search_transcript that no frontend code path could reach. The palette was a command list only.
new_effect: One search engine in src/ui.tsx serves both entry points. Real backends only: code_index.search for files and symbols, code_index.references for references, and the run_search catalog command for transcript, threads and tool output, whose hits return as a search_results Custom UiEvent. Six scopes, each declaring its source (code index or session log). The local tree walk survives only as an explicitly labelled fallback.
panel_changes: none. The Explorer field and the palette overlay both already existed; the second private implementation was deleted, not replaced by a panel.
modal_changes: The palette is still one overlay dialog with a focus trap. The catalog's own empty-query Search palette row was retired because from a bare palette gesture it carried no query and returned the head of the log; the input IS that command now.
keyboard_available: Mod+P opens it, arrows and Home and End move with clamping, Enter opens, Mod+Enter attaches to the running turn, Mod+Shift+Enter starts a side chat forked at the hit event. No result row needs a mouse or a per row button.
reversal_available: Not applicable to a read. The actions a hit can trigger (open, attach, side chat) are themselves non destructive.
evidence_visible: Every hit renders its provenance: path:line for a file hit, event id in session for a log hit, and the source it came from. Failing legs are surfaced per scope instead of swallowed, and the other legs still return.
duplicate_entry_removed: the private Explorer search implementation; the empty-query Search palette row; the separate search_transcript catalog row, collapsed into run_search (host.rs accepts run_search, search and search_transcript as aliases of one arm, so one capability keeps one command id)
errors_prevented: reading a filename fallback as though it were an index result; a palette Search entry that ran an empty query; a failed leg silently returning nothing; two catalog ids for one host search arm
still_unfinished: Semantic search is DEFERRED_MODEL_REQUIRED: include_semantic is pinned false and no scope offers it. Diff hunks and plan steps are not indexed anywhere, so those origins default to the scopes that really cover them rather than faking a scope. Correlation of a run_search answer is by echoed query text, so exactly one log leg is in flight at a time; a request id on the wire would make it exact.

previous_effect: No control existed. promote_run and resume_run_foreground were implemented on the host and absent from wire.ts CUSTOM_NAMES, so no frontend code could send them. A long task could not outlive the foreground run.
new_effect: Run in background resolves promote_run with the run and session ids, and the promoted job keeps running with no restart. Resume in foreground resolves resume_run_foreground with the durable job id. Pause, resume and stop deliberately reuse pause_run, resume_run and cancel_run, which already address a run by id, so no job verbs were invented.
panel_changes: The row lives in the EXISTING courtyard rail, collapsed, and unmounts when there is nothing running, so an idle courtyard is byte for byte the courtyard it was. The status bar gains a background work chip that exists only while work is moving. Neither is fixed chrome, so neither is counted as a permanent control.
keyboard_available: Through the palette for the underlying command ids. The row itself has no dedicated shortcut.
reversal_available: stop (cancel_run) and resume in foreground both address the same run or job, so a promotion is not one way.
evidence_visible: The row states phase, durable job id and last lifecycle event, the approval it is blocked on, the current verification read, and the newest process line. Each phase reads differently with styles off (idle, planning, running, paused, waiting on you, failed, done), so nothing is carried by colour.
duplicate_entry_removed: steer a background run and fork a background run were NOT added here: the composer already owns steer by run id and the timeline owns fork, so a second control would duplicate an existing action
errors_prevented: offering promote on a run that is already promoted, or resume on a run that is not paused; both are disabled with the reason rather than offered dead
still_unfinished: store.ts has NO jobs slice, so job lifecycle events arrive as Custom UiEvents and are read back with a regex over the first 200 characters of the notice JSON. That is marked in the source as a ponytail shortcut with the upgrade path (a jobs slice folded from those events). Job EXECUTION remains DEFERRED_MODEL_REQUIRED; create, list and cancel governance is what is ready.

previous_effect: Enter fired submit_turn with text only. Staged file attachments were chipped in the UI and DROPPED at submit even though submit_turn has always carried an attachments field on the contract. The voice mic recorded real audio through MediaRecorder and discarded it. The Chat composer Attach button had no onClick, and Send relabelled itself Queue turn while still firing submit_turn, for a queue the host does not have.
new_effect: Enter still sends one turn, and staged files are now real BlobRefs carrying the file name, a real SHA-256 content digest of the bytes, size and media type, so the contract field stops being dead. The submit control states what Enter will do right now (Start turn, Steer run, or Runtime down) and is also the submit menu.
panel_changes: none. The retired Attach slot now carries the composer state label, which is a one-for-one swap: same slot, more capability behind it. The two voice mics were removed outright.
keyboard_available: Enter for the default action, Mod+Enter to start a separate new turn while a run is live, Mod+/ to steer, Mod+Shift+N for a side chat. Every shortcut is read from the catalog, never invented by the surface.
reversal_available: none for a sent turn. cancel_run and pause_run address the run it starts.
evidence_visible: The mode label and its hint say exactly what Enter does and why it cannot send when the runtime is down. Attachment chips now correspond to bytes that are actually transmitted.
duplicate_entry_removed: the dead Chat Attach button, and the second of two identical voice mics
errors_prevented: silently dropping staged files at submit; a Queue turn promise with no host queue; a mic implying a transcription capability that does not exist in the catalog; starting an unrelated turn when a run was already in flight
still_unfinished: store.ts intentFor(submit_turn) still drops an attachments argument, so the courtyard composer builds that one Intent directly (the same shape the spine would build) rather than through runCommand. Threading one argument closes it.

previous_effect: PlanCard approve, edit step and reorder step each fired a real custom intent that was LOG-ONLY host side. Worse, the plan projection was frontend registry only with NO backend emitter, so the card had no feed and the domain was contract only end to end. This was the least mature high frequency domain in the baseline.
new_effect: hide-backend plan_domain.rs owns a durable, mutable PlanRecord persisted over the KV store, and store_and_publish is the single seam both the live turn emitter (host.rs around the turn kernel) and the mutation handlers route through, publishing a real plan projection patch. approve_plan, edit_plan_step, reorder_plan, skip_step and repair_step all resolve catalog commands that handle_plan_intent parses and re-publishes.
panel_changes: none. The per step actions live in a context menu reusing the shared popover styling, so no permanent control was added to the card.
keyboard_available: Through the palette for the command ids. The step menu opens from the step row.
reversal_available: Partial and honest: a failed step can be repaired, a step can be skipped with a stated blocker, and an edit replaces the step text. There is no plan level undo, and none is claimed.
evidence_visible: Each step renders its declared contract (acceptance predicate, allowed effects, related files, owner agent) beside its live state (status, verification, blocker) and the write gate. A write blocked step refuses every effectful verb and explains the block in words, not by colour.
errors_prevented: approving a plan the host never recorded; re-approving an approved step; editing or skipping a terminal step; repairing a step whose verification did not fail; dispatching an effectful verb on a write blocked step even if something asks for it
safety_cost: Skip requires a reason and sends it as the host blocker, so a skipped step cannot be silent. That is one extra required field, taken deliberately.
still_unfinished: WHETHER a plan appears is model dependent: PlanRecord::from_kernel reshapes a kernel plan, so with no model producing a plan the card renders nothing (which it does correctly). The card is bound and tested against real host shaped records, not against a live model run.

previous_effect: ContextStack pin, mute and evict fired pin_span and unpin_span, custom names with NO host handler at all. The whole memory domain (memory_add, memory_supersede, memory_record_outcome, memory_revalidate, memory_context, memory_get, memory_list) was built, durable, outcome governed and tested with no frontend route whatsoever. Save skill and three hardcoded load skill rows toasted success and persisted nothing.
new_effect: The memory stratum writes for real: marking a claim wrong records a negative outcome through memory_record_outcome so it self quarantines, the note field adds a durable memory through memory_add with the provenance the host requires, the same note supersedes the memory marked wrong through memory_supersede (keeping its history), and the stratum header revalidates this session's citations against disk through memory_revalidate.
panel_changes: The Skills stratum was retired outright (one save button and three hardcoded rows), and the per span pin and mute toggles that routed to pin_span went with them. The Memory stratum kept its rows and gained real writes.
keyboard_available: Through the palette for the memory command ids. The controls are in-panel toggles and a text field.
reversal_available: Yes, and it is the point of the domain: supersede writes an Active plus a Superseded record rather than deleting, so a replaced fact keeps its history and a wrong outcome is recoverable.
evidence_visible: The panel is a receipt: every row is something the host reported, including why each source is in the window from the packer's own numbers, what was excluded and why, and the blake3 address of each span. A claim marked wrong renders struck through before the correction is typed.
duplicate_entry_removed: the model row switch_profile button, an empty payload duplicate of the one model chooser
errors_prevented: pinning a span into a name with no handler; a Skills store that persisted nothing; deleting a fact instead of superseding it and losing the history
still_unfinished: memory_context, memory_get and memory_list remain read paths this panel reaches only through the manifest it already renders, not as their own controls.

previous_effect: ContextStack snapshot state claimed an RWKV state snapshot for instant resume and only called pushNotice: nothing was persisted. The fork state button beside it was titled fork this state (memcpy) and actually dispatched custom fleet_run with n:2, spawning two text task agents, which was LOG-ONLY anyway. Meanwhile checkpoint_create was implemented, host handled and blake3 integrity verified, reachable by a custom name that wire.ts refused.
new_effect: Snapshot resolves checkpoint_create and seals a real integrity verified boundary; fork resolves fork_session at the newest recorded step. The same checkpoint_create is reachable from the composer submit menu (Checkpoint this point, labelled from the composer text) and from the timeline history menu (Create checkpoint here, at the scrubbed step). Three entry points, one command, one meaning.
panel_changes: none. Both buttons already existed and were repointed.
keyboard_available: Through the palette for checkpoint_create, and through the composer submit menu.
reversal_available: Creating a checkpoint is additive, so nothing needs undoing. What it enables is the reversal surface: restore, replay, fork and the three rewinds all address the id it mints.
evidence_visible: The host mints the checkpoint id in a checkpoint_created Custom UiEvent, store.ts folds it into lastCheckpointId, and the timeline menu header shows that id. Before it exists, the dependent entries are disabled with the reason stated. This retired the regex readCheckpointId stopgap that used to scrape a truncated notice.
duplicate_entry_removed: the fleet_run misuse behind the fork button, and the memcpy claim in its label
errors_prevented: a toast reporting a snapshot that was never persisted; a fork label promising a constant size memcpy when the host replays a prefix under a new session id; addressing a checkpoint id scraped out of a truncated string
still_unfinished: The store keeps the LAST checkpoint id, not a list, so a user cannot pick among several checkpoints yet.

previous_effect: No control existed. A repo entered the host workspace graph UNTRUSTED by design, and while untrusted its instruction and policy files stay INERT. With no trust surface anywhere in the app, a repo's CLAUDE.md and policy had NO activation path at all. workspace_set_repo_trust was implemented on the host and unreachable.
new_effect: The add folder flow does not end at the folder. The menu stays open on an explicit trust decision, and workspace_set_repo_trust resolves with the repo id the host graph keys on (the folder name, as workspace_add_repo records it). Nothing is auto trusted.
panel_changes: none. The decision lives inside the existing add menu in the courtyard composer.
modal_changes: none. It is the second step of a menu the user already opened, not a new dialog.
keyboard_available: Through the same menu; workspace_set_repo_trust is a catalog command, so the palette resolves it too.
reversal_available: Yes: both states are offered and either can be sent again, so trust is not a one way door.
evidence_visible: Each choice states its security meaning rather than just a label (keep untrusted leaves instruction and policy files inert; trust makes them active for every run here), and the chip shows the current state including untrusted.
errors_prevented: adding a folder and silently getting an inert repo with no way to know why its instructions never applied; auto trusting a folder on the user's behalf
safety_cost: Adding a folder now takes two gestures instead of one, because the second gesture is a security decision. workspace_set_repo_trust also carries approval_policy ask, which the host enforces at the intent boundary, so granting trust is recorded and then parked on a gate. That is a deliberate cost, not a regression.
still_unfinished: No projection carries a trust VALUE back, so the status bar deliberately shows no repository trust indicator; the chip reflects the decision made in this session only. workspace_add_repo, workspace_repo, workspace_graph, add_environment and add_edge remain without their own controls.

previous_effect: Mod+S in the editor wrote through the fs connector AND fired a save_file custom intent alongside it. No host arm consumed save_file, so half the gesture was a log line. The connector write itself was a raw std::fs::write behind a workspace root check only, which means a frontend save bypassed the permission engine and the verifying applier that the agent's own edits are held to: the human had a wider write channel than the agent.
new_effect: One channel. The fs connector's write_file now dispatches edit.write_file through the same permission gated ToolDispatcher and verifying applier the agent uses, passing base_hash through when the caller supplies it so a concurrently changed file CONFLICTS instead of being clobbered. A refusal comes back as PolicyDenied and the editor shows save failed. The save_file custom intent was retired from the wire contract.
reversal_available: The write is a normal edit, so the diff review path (reject_diff, revert_diff) and a code rewind are what reverse it. The base_hash guard is the forward protection.
evidence_visible: A notice states saved path or save failed path. A refusal is never rendered as a success.
duplicate_entry_removed: the save_file custom intent that rode along with every save and reached no host arm; the second, unguarded write channel
errors_prevented: a frontend save escaping the effect policy the agent is held to; clobbering a file that changed under the open buffer; a gesture that half worked and logged the other half
safety_cost: REAL and deliberate: under the SHIPPED default policy (workspace_write_default = Ask) the dispatcher does not allow the write, so Mod+S is REFUSED and nothing is written, exactly like the agent's own edits under the same policy. That is a strictly worse save experience out of the box, taken on purpose, because the alternative is a human write path that outranks the policy. A test asserts the refusal and asserts nothing was written.
still_unfinished: The editor does not send base_hash yet (the connector passes it through when present), so the optimistic concurrency guard is available and unused from this surface. There is no in-app control to grant the write permission; that is a config decision, not a button.

rust_tests_now: 631 passed, 0 failed, across 15 hide crates (hide-backend 200, hide-protocol 26, hide-sdk 18, hide-serve 8, hide-kernel 74, hide-tools 62, hide-security 40, hide-verify 15, hide-state 26, hide-compat 39, hide-extension-registry 33, hide-program-runtime 34, hide-browser 19, hide-acp 25, hide-core 12)
catalog_honestly_refused: 1 (goal_get, rpc bound; runCommand throws a stated reason rather than pretending)
wire_custom_names: 33 before, 41 now (17 orphan names retired, 25 added)
permanent_controls: reviewed accounting: roughly 41 retirements, 2 genuinely new permanent visible elements (Terminal state row, per hunk detail toggle), 3 one-for-one swaps (StatusBar Branch button to Problems button, StateTimeline fork from here to history menu, Chat Attach slot to composer mode label)
git: HEAD is the base commit 4fbca8bc with ZERO commits; app/pnpm-lock.yaml and app/package.json unchanged
note: Catalog and test counts are from real runs, not from counting source. The rehydrated crates carry pre-existing long dashes that are byte identical to sealed pack 5a99d0e2 and were deliberately not touched.
